sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: ECS Transitions to Everforth ECS Amid ASGN Rebrand
feed.summary: ECS has officially changed its name to Everforth ECS, following the rebranding of its parent company, ASGN, to Everforth. This change unifies Everforth's business portfolio, enhancing its offerings in AI, cybersecurity, and enterprise IT modernization for defense, intelligence, and federal civilian agencies. The rebranding aims to provide expanded scale, specialized capabilities, and closer collaboration across the enterprise to meet complex mission challenges.

feed.title: Illinois Tool Works earnings ahead: Can margins stay elevated? By Investing.com
feed.summary: Illinois Tool Works (ITW) is set to release its first-quarter earnings, with analysts anticipating $2.57 EPS on $4.01 billion in revenue. Investors will focus on the company's ability to maintain high operating margins and pricing discipline amidst tepid revenue growth, especially given weakening volumes in some segments. Full-year guidance will be crucial, as the stock trades near its analyst consensus target with cautious sentiment.

feed.title: Walmart Inc. (WMT.US) Invests USD350M to Open Third Milk Processing Plant in US
feed.summary: Walmart Inc. (WMT.US) announced an investment of over USD350 million to establish its third milk processing plant in the United States. This new facility follows the opening of its first plant in Fort Wayne, Indiana, in 2018, and a second one in Valdosta, Georgia, in December of the previous year. The expansion underlines Walmart's continued focus on strengthening its dairy supply chain and production capabilities.

feed.title: NRC Renews License for Duke Energy (DUK)’s Robinson Nuclear Plant
feed.summary: The U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission has renewed the operating license for Duke Energy Corporation's H.B. Robinson Nuclear Plant for an additional 20 years, extending its life through 2050. This decision contributes to Duke Energy's ability to power approximately 570,000 homes. Analyst firms Morgan Stanley and Truist recently updated their ratings and price targets for Duke Energy, with Truist initiating coverage with a Buy rating due to the company's position to benefit from rising electricity demand from data centers.

feed.title: Union Pacific Corporation (UNP): The Best Infrastructure Stock to Buy with Highest Upside Potential
feed.summary: Union Pacific Corporation (UNP) is highlighted as a top infrastructure stock with high upside potential, following positive analyst revisions from Raymond James and Benchmark. The company reported strong first-quarter earnings, beating consensus estimates for EPS while narrowly topping revenue expectations, and reaffirmed its 2026 outlook for mid-single-digit earnings growth and operational improvements. Analysts anticipate that service enhancements, network efficiency, and potential acquisitions will drive increased profitability and market position for Union Pacific.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley Names Targa Resources (TRGP) a Top Pick
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has named Targa Resources (TRGP) its top pick in the midstream energy infrastructure sector, citing potential acceleration in Permian Basin gas production. Goldman Sachs also reiterated a Buy rating and raised its price target for TRGP due to strong sector performance and increasing natural gas demand. Targa Resources recently increased its quarterly dividend to $1.25 per share.

feed.title: ONEOK (OKE) Reports First-Quarter EPS of $1.23
feed.summary: ONEOK, Inc. (NYSE:OKE) reported first-quarter EPS of $1.23, falling short of consensus estimates but showing increased adjusted EBITDA. The company also raised its 2026 net income and adjusted EBITDA guidance, reflecting strong performance and an improved market outlook. Analysts at Scotiabank and Morgan Stanley have raised their price targets on OKE, maintaining Outperform and Overweight ratings, respectively.

feed.title: American Tower (AMT) Reports Q1 AFFO and Revenue Above Estimates
feed.summary: American Tower (NYSE:AMT) reported strong first-quarter results, with AFFO and revenue exceeding analyst expectations. The company also raised its full-year outlook, citing strong demand for digital infrastructure driven by mobile data usage, cloud adoption, and AI workloads. Mizuho upgraded American Tower to Outperform, noting that previous negatives appear priced in and highlighting improving tower fundamentals and an undervalued data center business.

feed.title: Barrick Mining says North American spinoff progressing
feed.summary: Barrick Mining plans to spin off its North American assets, including Nevada Gold Mines, into a new company by the end of 2026, aiming for an initial public offering primarily listed in New York. The new entity will have its own dedicated management team and aims to capitalize on significant growth potential from its roughly 2 million ounces of attributable gold production in 2025. Barrick is actively collaborating with Newmont Corp., its joint venture partner in Nevada Gold Mines, regarding the spinoff and potential inclusion of the Fourmile project.

feed.title: A leading Mass. biotech company opened a much anticipated lab and processing space. Here's what it looks like.
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific, a prominent Massachusetts-based biotech company, has inaugurated a new, highly anticipated lab and processing facility. The article provides a visual tour of this new space, showcasing its appearance through several images.

feed.title: Raymond James raises Tradeweb stock price target on swap strength
feed.summary: Raymond James has raised its price target for Tradeweb Markets (NASDAQ:TW) to $156 from $147, while maintaining an Outperform rating. The firm highlights Tradeweb's diversified multi-asset class platform and strength in interest rate swaps as key revenue drivers, with the company achieving eleven consecutive quarters of over 10% year-over-year revenue growth. Despite a recent stock price decline following strong Q1 2026 earnings, InvestingPro analysis suggests the stock is undervalued, with an attractive risk/reward profile.

feed.title: Goldman Sachs Upgrades Williams Companies (WMB) to Buy
feed.summary: Goldman Sachs upgraded The Williams Companies Inc. (WMB) to "Buy" from "Neutral" with an $82 price target, citing its strategically positioned Transcontinental Gas Pipeline and expected acceleration in natural gas transmission projects. Jefferies and RBC Capital also raised their price targets on WMB, highlighting the company's strong position to benefit from rising natural gas and electricity demand with limited commodity price exposure. The article suggests that while WMB has potential, certain AI stocks might offer greater upside and less downside risk.

feed.title: Mohawk Industries earnings loom as analysts question volume outlook By Investing.com
feed.summary: Mohawk Industries is set to announce its first-quarter earnings, with analysts keenly focused on whether the flooring manufacturer can maintain its growth amid signs of weakening demand and rising costs. Despite a "Buy" rating from analysts with an implied 19% upside, recent downgrades from Baird and BofA Securities to "Hold" reflect growing skepticism about the company's volume trends, pricing power, and ability to offset input cost inflation. The report will be crucial in determining if the company can navigate a challenging operating environment.

feed.title: ONEOK (OKE) Reports First-Quarter EPS of $1.23
feed.summary: ONEOK (NYSE:OKE) reported first-quarter EPS of $1.23, missing analyst estimates, but saw adjusted EBITDA rise to $1.997 billion. The company raised its 2026 net income and adjusted EBITDA guidance, while maintaining capital spending projections. Scotiabank and Morgan Stanley recently increased their price targets for ONEOK, maintaining positive ratings.

feed.title: Generac Holdings Inc. (NYSE : GNRC) acquired Allmand Bros., Inc.
feed.summary: Generac Holdings Inc. (NYSE: GNRC) announced the acquisition of Allmand Bros., Inc., with the transaction officially completing on January 5, 2026. This acquisition is part of Generac's broader strategy as it continues to expand its power generation solutions for residential, commercial, and industrial sectors. The company recently reported strong Q1 2026 earnings and raised its annual sales forecast, driven by demand for backup power and data centers.

feed.title: Air Products earnings up next as helium tailwinds build
feed.summary: Air Products and Chemicals Inc. (APD) is set to announce its fiscal second-quarter earnings, with analysts anticipating a boost from rising helium prices due to supply disruptions and strong hydrogen demand driven by the semiconductor industry. While the company is expected to report solid year-over-year growth in both EPS and revenue, investors will be closely watching its ability to sustain margin improvements and its disciplined approach to capital allocation, especially regarding large-scale clean energy projects. The stock currently holds a consensus "Buy" rating with an implied 5% upside from its current price.

feed.title: Leading biotech company launches its first US drug processing hub in Mass.
feed.summary: Thermo Fisher Scientific has opened its first U.S. bioprocess design center in Plainville, Massachusetts, expanding its existing 290,000-square-foot campus. This new 4,000-square-foot facility will provide biopharma and biotech customers with hands-on lab and training space to develop and scale biologics, filling 10 new positions. The center serves as the company's flagship U.S. location for drug development, despite recent job cuts in other Massachusetts operations.

feed.title: Deadline Alert: Apollo Global Management, Inc. (APO)
feed.summary: Glancy Prongay Wolke & Rotter LLP is reminding investors of Apollo Global Management, Inc. (APO) about a May 1, 2026 deadline to file as lead plaintiff in a securities fraud lawsuit. The lawsuit alleges that Apollo Global Management made misleading statements and failed to disclose material facts regarding the frequent communication between its leaders and Jeffrey Epstein, which negatively impacted the company's stock price after several news reports. Investors who purchased APO securities between May 10, 2021, and February 21, 2026, and suffered losses are urged to contact the law firm.

feed.title: VICI Properties faces earnings test after Q4 miss, major deal looms
feed.summary: VICI Properties is set to release its first-quarter results, with analysts expecting a significant rebound after a disappointing fourth-quarter miss. Investors will be closely watching the company's capital deployment strategy, including an upcoming $1.16 billion acquisition of seven casino properties from Golden Entertainment. Despite a current stock price below its 52-week high, analysts maintain a "Buy" consensus with a nearly 20% implied upside, underscoring confidence in VICI's long-term prospects.

feed.title: Cognizant Earnings: Expanded AI Partnerships Should Fuel Long-Term Growth
feed.summary: Cognizant Technology Solutions' first-quarter revenue grew 4% in constant currency, with bookings up 21%, bringing trailing 12-month bookings to a new high. The company is expanding its AI portfolio through partnerships and internal efforts like Project Leap, which are crucial for achieving its updated margin expansion goal. Morningstar maintains its $84 fair value estimate, viewing the stock as deeply undervalued due to Cognizant's comprehensive AI offerings and continuous business model evolution.

feed.title: Vanguard holds 74.08M Hewlett Packard Enterprise (NYSE: HPE) shares — 5.58%
feed.summary: Vanguard Portfolio Management has reported beneficial ownership of 74,077,721 shares of Hewlett Packard Enterprise Co (NYSE: HPE), which represents 5.58% of the common stock. This disclosure was made in a Schedule 13G filing on April 29, 2026, based on holdings as of March 31, 2026. Vanguard holds sole voting power for 296,051 shares and sole dispositive power for all 74,077,721 shares.

feed.title: Starr Safety Network Adds Honeywell FGV Approaches
feed.summary: Starr Safety Network has expanded its offerings by incorporating Honeywell FGV (Flight Guidance for Visual) Approaches into its safety program. This integration provides enhanced guidance for 100 airport runway ends, aiming to improve safety for pilots. This move strengthens Starr's commitment to aviation safety through technological advancements.

feed.title: Synopsys Set Price Surge: Here Is The Bullish Prediction
feed.summary: Synopsys (SNPS) is projected by 24/7 Wall St. to reach a price target of $597.11 within the next 12 months, representing a 23.4% upside with 90% confidence. This bullish prediction follows a strong Q1 FY2026 performance with significant revenue growth driven by the Ansys integration, a beat on non-GAAP EPS, and debt reduction. While long-term debt and U.S. export controls pose risks, the company's AI-driven backlog and successful Ansys synergies underpin the positive outlook.

feed.title: Carrier earnings on deck: Can data center boom offset residential drag?
feed.summary: Carrier Global Corp. is set to announce its first-quarter earnings, with investors focused on whether strong demand from data centers can counteract the ongoing challenges in residential HVAC markets. Analysts project earnings of 51 cents per share on $5 billion in revenue, indicating sequential improvement but year-over-year declines. The company's ability to convert its surging commercial HVAC orders, driven by AI infrastructure and hyperscale data centers, into revenue will be a key factor for its stock performance.

feed.title: ACNB Corporation Announces Second Quarter of 2026 Regular and Special Cash Dividends and New Share Repurchase Authorization
feed.summary: ACNB Corporation has announced an update to its capital allocation strategy. This includes an increase in its regular quarterly cash dividend to $0.42 per share, a one-time special cash dividend of $0.50 per share, and a new authorization to repurchase up to 310,000 shares of its common stock. These actions reflect the Board's confidence in the company's financial performance and commitment to shareholder value.

feed.title: Apellis earnings up next: Last report before Biogen takeover?
feed.summary: Apellis Pharmaceuticals is set to report its first-quarter earnings, which could be its last as an independent company before Biogen completes its $5.6 billion acquisition. Analysts anticipate a loss of 34 cents per share on revenue of $203.6 million, a reversal from the previous quarter's surprise profit. Investors will be seeking updates on the acquisition timeline and regulatory clearances, as well as insights into Syfovre sales trends and potential contingent value rights payouts.

feed.title: Newmont (NEM): The Best Precious Metal Stock to Buy According to Wall Street Analysts
feed.summary: Newmont Corporation (NEM) is highlighted as a top precious metal stock, with analysts raising price targets after its strong Q1 report. The company exceeded consensus estimates for Q1 adjusted EPS and revenue, producing significant amounts of gold, silver, and copper. Newmont also doubled its share repurchase program with an additional $6 billion authorization.

feed.title: Cardinal Health earnings on deck: Specialty growth in focus By Investing.com
feed.summary: Cardinal Health (CAH) is set to report its third-quarter fiscal 2026 earnings, with analysts focusing on the company's specialty segment performance and its transformation strategy. The company is expected to report EPS of $2.79 on revenue of $62.11 billion, reflecting significant year-over-year growth and confidence in its shift towards high-growth healthcare services. Investors will be particularly watching for continued momentum in advanced therapies, recent acquisitions, and any updates to full-year guidance.

feed.title: Lennox International (NYSE:LII) Shares Up 7.6% - Here's What Happened
feed.summary: Lennox International (NYSE:LII) shares surged 7.6% after reporting Q1 earnings per share (EPS) of $3.35, beating the Zacks consensus estimate, and a 6% revenue increase to $1.1 billion. Despite the positive EPS surprise, the quarter saw a mixed performance with operating income and GAAP EPS declining year-over-year. Analysts have a "Hold" consensus rating with an average price target of $563.67, with some positive revisions from Oppenheimer and some downgrades from other firms.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials (VMC) Q1 2026 Earnings Transcript
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials reported a strong start to 2026 with a 9% increase in adjusted EBITDA, driven by gross profit margin expansion and lower SAG expenses. The company reiterated its full-year adjusted EBITDA guidance of $2.4 billion to $2.6 billion, despite anticipating short-term margin pressure from elevated diesel costs, particularly in Q2. Strategic investments in greenfield projects and an advantaged geographic footprint, coupled with robust public infrastructure and nonresidential demand, are expected to support continued growth and pricing power.

feed.title: Why General Motors (GM) Shares Are Trading Lower Today
feed.summary: General Motors (GM) shares dropped 4% following a mixed first-quarter report, which included lower U.S. sales, a reduced full-year net income forecast, and delays in its electric vehicle initiatives. Despite exceeding earnings and revenue estimates, these concerns, coupled with a significant charge related to a revised EV strategy, outweighed the positive financial performance. This decline follows a recent gain due to the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting market sensitivity to company-specific news.

feed.title: WM’s mixed Q1 led by strong core business trends, health care improvements
feed.summary: WM (Waste Management) reported mixed Q1 2026 results, with strong core business trends and improvements in its healthcare sector offsetting lower overall volumes due to severe winter weather and intentional shedding of lower-margin business. The company saw robust core price growth and an increase in recycling volumes due to facility upgrades, while also making significant strides in renewable energy production. WM reaffirmed its full-year financial outlook, highlighting positive developments in its sustainability efforts and the ongoing integration of Stericycle.

feed.title: UBS Raises its Price Target on Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) to $74
feed.summary: UBS has increased its price target for Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) to $74 from $66, maintaining a Buy rating, following the company's Q1 adjusted EPS beat and revenue growth despite operational challenges in Indonesia. In contrast, Morgan Stanley downgraded FCX to Equal Weight, citing a slower production ramp-up and higher costs at the Grasberg Mine, while Jefferies also lowered its price target slightly due to similar concerns but still views FCX favorably for copper exposure. Freeport CEO Kathleen Quirk highlighted the company's strong financial performance and ongoing efforts to restore operations and improve efficiency.

feed.title: Stocks making big moves yesterday: Commvault, Atlas Energy Solutions, CECO Environmental, Centene, and LifeStance Health Group
feed.summary: Several stocks made significant moves yesterday, April 29, 2026, driven by strong earnings, positive guidance, or market index inclusion. Commvault, CECO Environmental, and Centene all saw their shares rise after reporting better-than-expected first-quarter 2026 results and, in some cases, raising full-year guidance. Atlas Energy Solutions benefited from a spike in crude oil prices, while LifeStance Health Group gained entry into the S&P SmallCap 600 index.

feed.title: How JPMorgan's CIO is reshaping work at the bank with a $19.8 billion annual tech and AI budget
feed.summary: Lori Beer, JPMorgan Chase's global CIO, is spearheading the integration of AI agents into the bank's operations, backed by a significant $19.8 billion annual tech and AI budget. Her strategy focuses on carefully defining AI agent roles, managing data access, and ensuring human oversight, especially in critical areas like software engineering and asset management. The bank is also investing in proprietary AI tools and redesigning workflows to maximize productivity and address potential job displacement, emphasizing a balance between innovation and risk.

feed.title: Anika Therapeutics Posts Strong Q1 2026 Revenue Growth
feed.summary: Anika Therapeutics reported a robust Q1 2026 with revenue up 13% to $29.6 million and adjusted EBITDA climbing to $4.3 million, driven by strong growth in Commercial and OEM channels and improved gross margins. The company reaffirmed its full-year 2026 guidance for top-line growth and adjusted EBITDA margins between 5% and 10, completing a $15 million share repurchase and preparing for a board restructure. Analysts currently rate ANIK stock as a Buy with a $17.00 price target, while TipRanks' AI Analyst, Spark, rates it Neutral.

feed.title: Uber moves toward becoming an ‘everything app’ with hotel bookings powered by Expedia
feed.summary: Uber is expanding its app services to include hotel bookings, partnering with Expedia Group to offer 700,000 hotels and other properties globally, with Vrbo vacation rentals to follow. This move is part of Uber's strategy to become an "everything app," aiming to consolidate various customer needs into a single platform. Uber One members will receive exclusive discounts and credits on hotel bookings.

feed.title: Sysco Reports Strong Fiscal Third-Quarter Results, Announces Acquisition Plan
feed.summary: Sysco reported strong fiscal third-quarter results for 2026, driven by improved case volume trends, strengthened gross margin performance, and disciplined operational execution. CEO Kevin M. Hourican expressed confidence in achieving the high end of the company's full-year adjusted earnings per share guidance and introduced Interim CFO Brandon Sewell. The company also confirmed its strategic acquisition of Jetro Restaurant Depot to enter the cash-and-carry space.

feed.title: Invitation Homes earnings on deck as margins face pressure By Investing.com
feed.summary: Invitation Homes (INVH) is expected to report first-quarter earnings with a projected 34% decline year-over-year despite a slight revenue increase, indicating margin pressure. This comes as single-family rent growth has slowed and regulatory scrutiny over institutional landlords increases. The company is pivoting towards share repurchases and build-to-rent acquisitions, but investors are questioning the sustainability of its profit margins amidst these challenges.

feed.title: Bunge profit plunges 66% despite revenue surge as Viterra integration weighs
feed.summary: Bunge, a global agribusiness and food company, reported a 66% plunge in profit despite a revenue surge, largely due to the integration of Viterra. The company has revised its adjusted earnings forecast for the full year to $9 to $9.50 per share, an increase from its previous prediction.

feed.title: Veralto Q1 Earnings & Revenues Beat Estimates, Increase Y/Y
feed.summary: Veralto (VLTO) reported strong first-quarter 2026 results, surpassing Zacks Consensus Estimates for both earnings and revenues. The company's adjusted EPS rose 12.6% year-over-year to $1.07, and total revenues increased 6.8% to $1.42 billion. Veralto also provided a positive outlook for Q2 and full-year 2026, projecting continued core sales growth and increased adjusted EPS.

feed.title: Omnicom's Q1 Earnings Miss Estimates, Increase Year Over Year
feed.summary: Omnicom (OMC) reported mixed first-quarter 2026 results, with earnings per share of $1.90 missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.91 but increasing 11.8% year over year. Total revenues, however, surpassed estimates at $6.2 billion, marking a significant 69.2% rise from the previous year. The company's adjusted EBITA also saw substantial growth, increasing by 69.5% to $861.4 million.

feed.title: Why General Motors (GM) Shares Are Trading Lower Today
feed.summary: General Motors (GM) shares dropped 4% following a mixed first-quarter report. The company experienced a decline in U.S. sales, lowered its full-year net income forecast, and announced setbacks in its electric vehicle plans, including a $6 billion charge for revised EV strategy. These concerns overshadowed an earnings and revenue beat, leading to the stock's decline.

feed.title: Verisk Analytics (NASDAQ:VRSK) Posts Earnings Results, Beats Estimates By $0.08 EPS
feed.summary: Verisk Analytics (NASDAQ:VRSK) reported strong Q1 earnings, beating analyst estimates with an EPS of $1.82 and revenue of $782.6 million. The company announced a $1.5 billion accelerated share repurchase program, an 11% dividend increase, and issued FY2026 EPS guidance of $7.45–7.75, emphasizing accelerated AI-driven product innovation. Despite near-term headwinds such as lower weather-related activity and a federal contract work stoppage, management expects gradual revenue improvement throughout the year.

feed.title: U.S. Bancorp (NYSE:USB) Price Target Raised to $74.00
feed.summary: Oppenheimer has increased its price target for U.S. Bancorp (NYSE:USB) to $74.00, reiterating an "outperform" rating and implying a 32.46% upside. This comes after the financial services provider reported better-than-expected earnings of $1.18 per share on revenues of $7.32 billion, though some analysts have trimmed their targets. Despite insider selling, 77.60% of the stock is held by institutional investors.

feed.title: RTX Space Interceptor Win Adds New Layer To Valuation Debate
feed.summary: RTX (NYSE:RTX) has secured a contract from the U.S. Space Force for the design and development of space-based interceptor prototypes, enhancing its role in next-generation missile defense. This win introduces a new dimension to RTX's valuation, moving beyond its traditional focus on earnings and backlog. While the company has seen strong stock performance, investors should monitor the program's progress and consider existing risks like debt and insider selling.

feed.title: Cincinnati Financial (CINF) Profit Swing Challenges Bearish Catastrophe Risk Narrative
feed.summary: Cincinnati Financial (CINF) reported a significant profit swing in Q1 2026, with net income of US$274 million compared to a loss in Q1 2025, and trailing twelve-month earnings growth of 90.5%. This strong recent profitability, marked by a net profit margin of 21.3%, challenges bearish narratives regarding rising catastrophe risks and potential margin compression, despite analyst forecasts predicting future revenue and earnings pressure. The article highlights a disconnect between the company's robust recent financial performance and more cautious long-term analyst expectations, particularly concerning potential earnings decline and a share price above DCF fair value.

feed.title: Iron Mountain earnings up next: Can data centers fuel growth? By Investing.com
feed.summary: Iron Mountain is set to release its first-quarter earnings, with analysts anticipating a significant increase in EPS and revenue, driven by its data center business. Investors are focusing on whether the company's data center and Asset Lifecycle Management segments can fuel growth, alongside the stable core Global Records and Information Management business. The company's stock has seen a strong performance, and the earnings report will be crucial in validating its growth strategy and current valuation.

feed.title: Vanguard reports 18.7M shares of Consolidated Edison (ED) — 5.07% stake
feed.summary: Vanguard Portfolio Management has reported beneficial ownership of 18,690,590 shares of Consolidated Edison Inc. common stock, representing a 5.07% stake in the company as of March 31, 2026. This disclosure, made via a Schedule 13G filing, indicates sole dispositive power over all shares and sole voting power for 48,606 shares. The filing is a passive investment disclosure, reflecting ownership reporting rather than an active change in strategy.

feed.title: Phillips 66 surges after unexpected Q1 profit on higher refining margins (PSX:NYSE)
feed.summary: Phillips 66 (PSX) stock rose significantly in Wednesday's trading, becoming one of the top gainers in the oil and gas sector on the S&P 500. The surge followed a surprise Q1 profit announcement, primarily attributed to higher refining margins. This unexpected financial performance has boosted the company's stock value.
